title: fix: lines_code and lines_comment always computed as 0 in engine.rs
description:
lines_code, lines_comment, and lines_blank in MetricValues are always 0. The count_lines() function in metrics.rs returns placeholder zeros rather than real values.

Approach: use tree-sitter AST node classification (not raw line scanning) to avoid ambiguity with multi-line strings and doc comments.
- Walk all nodes in the file's parse tree
- Lines containing at least one comment node and no non-comment, non-whitespace nodes → lines_comment
- Lines containing at least one named non-comment node → lines_code
- Remaining non-empty lines → lines_blank
- Count distinct line numbers across all matching nodes

Acceptance examples:
- A file with 3 // comment lines, 10 code lines, 2 blank lines → reports lines_comment=3, lines_code=10, lines_blank=2
- A line with both code and a trailing comment counts as lines_code, not lines_comment
- The sum lines_code + lines_comment + lines_blank must equal the file's total non-empty line count

title: fix: cognitive complexity overcounts chained logical operators due to sequence_id always None
description: metrics.rs:84-96 deduplicates boolean-operator branches via sequence_id, but engine.rs:346-351 always sets sequence_id: None on every Branch it creates. The dedup arm (LogicalAnd|LogicalOr, Some(id)) is dead code. Each 'and'/'or' in 'a and b and c' counts as a separate branch instead of once, overcounting cognitive complexity. Fix: in engine.rs, assign a shared sequence_id to all sibling and/or nodes in the same boolean-operator chain.

title: fix: file_lines_max threshold computed but never checked at file level
description: EffectiveThresholds includes file_lines_max but analyze_path() in main.rs never checks module.lines_total against it. Per-file line count violations never fire. Fix: check module.lines_total against thresholds.file_lines_max in analyze_path() and surface violations in FileReport.

title: fix: ABC metric is computed and serialized but never checked against any threshold
description:
abc metric (MetricValues.abc) is computed, stored, and serialized but never checked against any threshold — no push_limit_violation call exists for it.

Fix path: add enforcement.
1. Add abc_max: Option<u32> to AppThresholds in config.rs
2. Add it to EffectiveThresholds and populate in for_role()
3. Call push_limit_violation for 'abc' in build_unit_report() when unit.metrics.abc > abc_max
4. Add a test: a function with ABC > threshold must produce a LimitViolation finding

Acceptance: a file with a function that exceeds abc_max produces a 'abc' violation in the report output.

title: fix: exported-threshold violations (exported_cyclomatic_max etc.) never fire
description: EffectiveThresholds::for_role() computes exported_* thresholds for library-role files, but build_unit_report() in main.rs:414-461 never calls push_limit_violation() for them. Library files can never produce exported-threshold violations regardless of how high their exported metrics climb. Fix: add push_limit_violation calls for exported_* fields when unit.is_exported and the threshold is Some(x).

title: Implement feedback loop: persist violation history (pretender-5rk)
description:
## Problem

pretender calculates metrics (cyclomatic complexity, cognitive complexity, nesting, duplication) but doesn't persist them. Agents can't see trends or recurring violations across sessions. This makes pretender's output ephemeral — there's no reason to keep using it beyond a single check.

## Scope

This ticket covers **implementation only**. The design decisions are in pretender-42d (Design doc: violation feedback loop). Implement based on that design doc's recommendations.

## Implementation

- Persist check results (violations, per-function metrics) to `.pretender/history/`
- `pretender report` shows trends over time (e.g., violation count per week, per metric)
- JSON output includes a `trends` field with recurring-violation signals

## Integration Points (deferred to separate tickets)

- openspec constraint generation: not in scope — see follow-up ticket
- Agent prompt injection: not in scope — see follow-up ticket

## Acceptance Criteria

- `pretender check .` writes results to `.pretender/history/events.jsonl`
- Running `pretender check .` 3 times with different files produces 3 entries in the history
- `pretender report --format json | jq '.history.trends'` shows a non-empty trends array
- All existing tests still pass
- Tests exist for the persistence layer (mocked timestamps)

## Dependencies

- Depends on pretender-42d (design doc) — design decisions must be approved before coding

title: Fix pretender check CLI: default to repo root when no path given
description:
## Problem

`pretender check` with no path argument fails with an error. Session data shows agents repeatedly hitting this: they run `pretender check` (no path) and get an error, then either look up help or give up. The tool should default to scanning the repo root when no paths are provided.

## Expected

- `pretender check` (no args) → scans current directory (`.`) by default
- `pretender check .` → same behavior (backward compatible)
- Error message if no path and no pretender.toml found (guidance: "No paths given and no pretender.toml found. Run 'pretender init' to configure, or pass paths explicitly.")

## Scope

This is a CLI change in the clap command definition for the `check` subcommand. The `paths` argument should become optional with a default of `["."]` or the current directory.

## Impact

This is the #2 highest-leverage fix (after wai way integration). It removes the most common CLI friction for agents.

## Acceptance Criteria

- `pretender check` without arguments defaults to `"."`
- `pretender complexity` without arguments defaults to `"."`
- `pretender duplication` without arguments defaults to `"."`
- Tests verify the default behavior
- Help text clearly shows the argument is optional

title: Add check_pretender() to wai way
description:
## Problem

`wai way` is the standard project initialization workflow — it runs 16 check_*() functions that diagnose project aspects and suggest setup. pretender is completely absent from this system. Every other tool in the charly ecosystem (dont, testaruda, openspec, beads) has a path to discovery through wai; pretender doesn't.

## Expected

Add a `check_pretender()` function to `wai/src/commands/way/` that:

1. Detects whether `pretender.toml` exists at the repo root
2. If missing, suggests running `pretender init --non-interactive`
3. Explains the value: "Structural code quality checks (cyclomatic complexity, cognitive complexity, nesting, duplication) for all supported languages"
4. Returns Status::Info (recommendation, not required)

## Implementation

This is a single Rust function (~50 lines) following the pattern of existing checks like `check_editorconfig()` or `check_typos()`. See `wai/src/commands/way/mod.rs` for the pattern.

## References

- `wai/src/commands/way/mod.rs` — the vec![...] of checks that needs the new entry
- `wai/src/commands/way/linting.rs` — example of a check function pattern

## Acceptance Criteria

- `wai way` output includes a "Code quality" check (or similar) that detects missing pretender.toml
- Running `wai way` in a repo without pretender.toml shows a recommendation to run `pretender init`
- Running `wai way` in a repo with pretender.toml shows a pass
- Tests exist for both cases
notes:
## Repo Location

This work happens in the **wai repo**, not the pretender repo:
  cd /var/home/sasha/para/areas/dev/gh/charly/wai
  src/commands/way/mod.rs  — the vec![...] of checks
  src/commands/way/linting.rs  — example check function pattern

After adding the function, register it in the `checks` vec! in mod.rs and add tests.

title: F18: pretender — CI should call 'just ci' instead of inlining cargo commands (single source of truth)
description:
Source: CLI consistency audit F18

Problem: pretender's ci.yml inlines "cargo fmt -- --check", "cargo check", "cargo clippy -- -D warnings", "cargo test" directly in the YAML, even though pretender's justfile has a "ci" recipe that goes unused by CI. This is a single-source-of-truth violation: updating the local "just ci" recipe does not affect what gates PRs.

Expected: Replace the inlined cargo commands with a single "just ci" invocation in ci.yml, matching wai and dont's pattern.

Files: pretender/.github/workflows/ci.yml — replace the four cargo run steps with a single "run: just ci" step.

Reference: wai/.github/workflows/ci.yml is the canonical template.

Verification: "grep 'just ci' pretender/.github/workflows/ci.yml" should match.

Classification: AFK (agent-friendly).

title: F24: pretender — fix .gitignore broken glob (pretender/.pretender/ -> .pretender/), runtime cache accidentally committed
description:
Source: CLI consistency audit F24

Problem: pretender's .gitignore says "pretender/.pretender/" but the actual runtime-cache directory is ".pretender/" at the repo root. The glob has an extra "pretender/" prefix that doesn't correspond to any real path, so it silently matches nothing. Three runtime-state files (.pretender/history/events.jsonl, .pretender/history/summaries.json, .pretender/last-check.json) are accidentally committed to git history.

Expected: Fix the glob to ".pretender/" (or "/.pretender/" to anchor at repo root). Then decide deliberately whether .pretender/history/events.jsonl should be tracked (team-shared metrics, matching beads/dont pattern) or ignored (per-machine cache, matching testaruda).

Files: pretender/.gitignore — change "pretender/.pretender/" to ".pretender/".

Verification: "git -C pretender check-ignore .pretender/" should return the path. "git -C pretender ls-files .pretender" should show no committed files (or only deliberately tracked ones).

Classification: AFK (agent-friendly).

title: check crashes on binary files instead of skipping them
description: Running `pretender check .` on a directory that contains binary files (e.g. compiled artifacts in target/) crashes with 'stream did not contain valid UTF-8' instead of gracefully skipping the file. The tool should detect non-text files and skip them with an optional warning.
notes: Verify: `pretender check .` on a directory containing binary files (e.g. target/) completes without error and exits 0. Binary files should be silently skipped or produce a warning line, not a crash.

title: Cleanup baseline/spec mismatch and broken generated artifacts
description:
## Why
The repository is green, but specs and generated artifacts overclaim implemented functionality. This causes misleading baselines and produces broken outputs like hooks/workflows that call unsupported flags.

## What
- reconcile baseline OpenSpec docs with current implemented MVP
- stop generating broken hook/workflow behavior or degrade it clearly
- clean up repo metadata/documentation surfaced by the review

## Acceptance Criteria
- update-mvp-spec-baseline passes strict validation
- generated hook/CI outputs no longer claim unsupported behavior silently
- repo quality gates still pass after cleanup
- any remaining follow-up is captured in beads
close_reason: Reconciled the OpenSpec baseline with the implemented MVP, archived update-mvp-spec-baseline, fixed generated hook/workflow outputs, persisted report cache for all successful checks, and refreshed top-level documentation.

title: fix: validate_percent() in config.rs accepts values >= 101 instead of rejecting them
close_reason: False positive: validate_percent uses u32 and the condition is correct.

title: fix: collect_input_files() ignores config.pretender.exclude patterns
description: collect_input_files() in main.rs:385-412 never filters paths against config.pretender.exclude. Patterns like vendor/**, node_modules/**, and **/*_generated.* are in the default exclude list but are silently analyzed anyway. Fix: build a GlobSet from config.pretender.exclude at the start of collect_input_files() and skip any path that matches.

title: fix: get_parser() called per-file inside par_iter(), rebuilding registry for every file
description: get_parser() is called inside a par_iter() closure in main.rs:282-286, creating a fresh ParserRegistry for every file processed in parallel. Registry construction is O(n) work repeated per file instead of once. Fix: build the registry once in run() and pass it (or a reference to the matched parser) into analyze_path().
close_reason: merged into pretender-5wh: both defects are in the same function and require a single coordinated fix

title: fix: get_parser() builds dead registry and is called per-file inside par_iter()
description:
Two related defects in get_parser() / main.rs that should be fixed together:

1. Dead registry (main.rs:539-558): get_parser() constructs a ParserRegistry, queries it, discards the result (_p), then runs a second parallel match on the extension. The registry result is never used.

2. Per-file rebuilding (main.rs:282-286): get_parser() is called inside par_iter(), so a new ParserRegistry is constructed for every file analyzed in parallel. Registry construction is O(n) repeated work.

Fix: remove ParserRegistry from get_parser() entirely. Build the registry once in run() and resolve the parser before entering par_iter(). Pass the resolved parser (or language) into analyze_path() directly. This eliminates both the dead work and the repeated allocation.

Acceptance:
- ParserRegistry is constructed exactly once per invocation
- get_parser() no longer references ParserRegistry
- analyze_path() receives the resolved parser, not a file extension
- Existing analysis output is unchanged

title: Fix clippy dead-code failure in Python plugin manifest helpers
description:
## Why
`just ci` currently fails on main during `cargo clippy -- -D warnings` because `pretender/src/python.rs` defines an unused `PYTHON_MANIFEST` constant and `PythonParser::manifest()` helper. This blocks the verify-close quality gate and leaves the repo red.

## What
Remove or wire up the unused Python manifest helper so clippy passes again, then rerun `just ci`.

## Acceptance Criteria
- `cargo clippy -- -D warnings` passes on main
- `just ci` passes locally
- Python plugin manifest loading still works for the intended code path (or dead code is removed)
close_reason: Removed unused Python manifest helpers from pretender/src/python.rs; manifest parsing remains covered in plugin tests. just ci now passes locally.

title: Tracer bullet: cyclomatic complexity on one Python file to stdout
description: Thin vertical slice proving the architecture works end-to-end before building the full CLI. Parse tests/fixtures/python_simple.py via tree-sitter, map to universal model, compute cyclomatic complexity for each CodeUnit, print sorted results to stdout. Cuts through: Cargo workspace (pretender-h6v), universal model types (pretender-b5o), query engine (pretender-6xq), Python plugin (pretender-07m), and cyclomatic metric (pretender-jy9). No config, no roles, no modes, no file output. If the architecture has a structural flaw it surfaces here — before 10 more tickets build on it. Verify: cargo run -- complexity tests/fixtures/python_simple.py prints at least one result with function name and score, exits 0.

title: Native pre-commit hook generator
description: pretender hooks install: write .git/hooks/pre-commit shim: #!/usr/bin/env sh\nexec pretender check --staged --diff-only\nMake executable. pretender hooks uninstall: remove the shim. Hook must complete <2s on normal commits — diff-only mode is the key constraint.
notes: Implemented pretender hooks install/uninstall with a Pretender-managed pre-commit shim. Current shim intentionally falls back to repo-wide pretender check . and documents future diff-only support in pretender-a80.
close_reason: install/uninstall implemented with safety guards; 6 tests; shim uses --staged and is built from marker constant

title: CLI: pretender check command with human + JSON output
description: Main scan entrypoint. Flags: [paths], --staged, --diff-only, --diff-base, --format (human|json|sarif|junit|markdown), --output. Reads config, detects languages, runs all enabled metrics, applies per-role thresholds, emits colored terminal output (human) or JSON. Exit code: 0 unless gate mode with red violations. Parallelize file processing with rayon.
notes: Implemented a first vertical slice of pretender check: paths + --format human|json, default/optional pretender.toml loading, recursive file collection, parser dispatch, role detection, metric computation, and threshold violations. Remaining scope: staged/diff flags, diff-base, output file routing, sarif/junit/markdown formats, gate-mode exit semantics, and rayon parallelism.
close_reason: check command MVP slice complete: --output flag, exit code on violations, color-gated human output with violation lines, rayon parallelization with deterministic ordering. 9 cli integration tests + 35 unit tests pass.

title: CLI: pretender init command
description: Interactive wizard using dialoguer or similar. Prompts: mode (guidance/tiered/gate), language detection (auto/explicit list), install pre-commit hook (y/n), generate GitHub Actions workflow (y/n). Writes pretender.toml, optionally writes .git/hooks/pre-commit shim and .github/workflows/pretender.yml.
notes: GitHub Actions generation prompt is stubbed in V0 — actual YAML generation lives in pretender-fb3 (P2). In V0, selecting 'generate GitHub Actions' writes a placeholder comment in pretender.toml: # CI: run pretender ci generate github when pretender-fb3 is implemented. Do not block V0 init on fb3. Verify: pretender init in a temp dir writes pretender.toml with correct mode/language/roles structure and a working .git/hooks/pre-commit shim.

title: Role detection: path glob matching
description: Implement role detection layer: read [roles] section from pretender.toml, match file paths against glob patterns, assign app/library/test/script/generated/vendor role. Priority order: (1) explicit pragma in file comment, (2) path globs from config, (3) heuristics. Per-role threshold overrides must be applied before metric evaluation.
close_reason: Implemented role detection with pragma, glob, heuristic precedence and effective thresholds

title: Config: parse pretender.toml with serde + toml
description: Implement config parsing for pretender.toml. Full schema from openspec/specs/cli-and-config.md: [pretender], [thresholds], [thresholds.test], [thresholds.library], [thresholds.script], [bands], [scope], [execute], [plugins], [output], [roles]. Validate config on load, emit miette diagnostics for errors. Defaults align with established conventions (SonarQube, McCabe, Google/MS style guides).
close_reason: Implemented pretender.toml parsing with defaults and validation diagnostics

title: JavaScript/TypeScript language plugins
description: Write languages/javascript/metrics.scm and languages/typescript/metrics.scm. Capture: function_declaration, arrow_function, method_definition, if_statement, ternary_expression, logical_expression (&& ||), switch_case, catch_clause, for_statement, while_statement. Smell weights: eval=5.0, Function=4.0 (dynamic). TypeScript plugin can extend JS plugin.
notes: TypeScript plugin references the JavaScript grammar and adds TS-specific captures in its own .scm file — no grammar-level inheritance. TS-specific captures to add: type_annotation (for parameter types), interface_declaration (treat as a CodeUnit with kind=Initializer), generic_type (no metric impact, capture for completeness). The JS .scm file is the base; TS .scm imports or duplicates JS captures and extends them. Verify: both plugins produce correct cyclomatic scores on tests/fixtures/js_sample.js and tests/fixtures/ts_sample.ts respectively.

title: Python language plugin (.scm + plugin.toml)
description: Write languages/python/metrics.scm capturing: function_definition, if_statement, elif_clause, while_statement, for_statement, try_statement, except_clause, conditional_expression, boolean_operator. Write plugin.toml with branches weights and assertion patterns (assert, self.assert*, pytest.raises, pytest.warns).
close_reason: Moved queries/python.scm to languages/python/metrics.scm and added plugin.toml manifest with branch weights + assertion patterns; PluginManifest scaffolding loads and parses but is not yet wired into the engine.

title: Tree-sitter query engine: parse .scm files into universal model
description: Build the adapter layer that: (1) loads a tree-sitter grammar via tree-sitter-loader, (2) runs a .scm query file against a parsed CST, (3) maps @captures to universal model types (CodeUnit, Block, Branch, etc.). Start with Python grammar. Capture name conventions defined in openspec/specs/universal-code-model.md.
notes: Verify: cargo test -p pretender-engine parse_python_fixture green. Test: given tests/fixtures/python_simple.py (a Python file with at least 2 functions), the engine must produce a Module with >=2 CodeUnits, each with correct span and branch captures. On tree-sitter parse errors: emit diagnostic on stderr (file path + error span) and skip the file — never emit a partial Module.
close_reason: Completed: Python parser now delegates to QueryEngine using queries/python.scm; engine and CLI tests are green.

title: Implement universal code model types
description: Implement Module, CodeUnit, Block, Node, Branch, BranchKind, CallSite, Parameter, Span, Import structs/enums as specified in openspec/specs/universal-code-model.md. Pure data types with no logic. Include serde Serialize/Deserialize derives for JSON output.
notes: Also create tests/fixtures/ directory with: python_simple.py (2+ functions of known cyclomatic complexity), rust_simple.rs, js_simple.js. Each fixture file must have a comment at the top declaring expected complexity scores so tests can assert against known values. Grammar downloads must be pre-fetched in test setup; tests must not make network calls — use a local grammar cache dir seeded via test fixture setup. Verify: cargo test -p pretender-core tests green.
close_reason: Implemented universal code model serde data types and fixtures

title: Implement pure metric functions
description: Implement cyclomatic, cognitive, function_lines, params, nesting_max, and abc metric functions as pure functions over CodeUnit. Each function takes &CodeUnit and returns a numeric value. Full unit test coverage — these are trivially testable.
close_reason: Implemented pure metric functions with unit coverage

title: Initialize Cargo workspace and project skeleton
description: Set up Cargo.toml with all dependencies from the design doc: tree-sitter, tree-sitter-loader, clap, serde, toml, serde_sarif, git2, rayon, indicatif, miette, thiserror, anyhow. Create lib.rs and main.rs stubs. Verify `cargo check` passes.

title: Design doc: violation feedback loop for recurring structural violations
description:
## Problem

The feedback loop (tracking recurring structural violations to surface as constraints) was designed conceptually in pretender-5rk but never produced a concrete design document. Before any code is written, the design questions must be answered and reviewed.

## Design Questions

The design doc at `pretender/docs/feedback-loop-design.md` should answer:

1. **Violation taxonomy**: What taxonomy to use? (pretender's existing threshold categories vs. something richer)
2. **Aggregation strategy**: How to aggregate — per project, per agent model, per code area?
3. **Auto-generation**: Should recurring violations auto-generate openspec constraints or pretender profile defaults?
4. **Threshold**: How many occurrences before a pattern is "recurring"?

## Starting Points

- `pretender/src/config.rs:166-260` — threshold definitions
- `pretender/src/metrics.rs:6-45` — metric calculations
- `pretender/src/main.rs:44-73` — main entry point
- `pretender/src/roles.rs:171-231` — EffectiveThresholds

## Deliverable

A design document at `pretender/docs/feedback-loop-design.md` with all 4 questions answered, reviewed and approved.

## Acceptance Criteria

- Design doc covers all 4 questions with clear recommendations
- Design doc is reviewed and approved
- Implementation ticket is created from the design (or pretender-3nh is updated with the design decisions)
close_reason: Design doc written at docs/feedback-loop-design.md. Key finding: the feedback loop is already implemented in V1 (history module exists, integrated, tested). Phase 2 scope defined.

title: Research: measure indirect pretender usage via justfile/CI wrappers
description:
## Problem

The adoption research only counted direct pretender invocations in pi session logs. Agents may use pretender indirectly through `just complexity`, `just check`, `just ci`, or pre-commit hooks. These invocations don't show up in pi session logs as "pretender" calls.

## Research Question

How many projects wrap pretender in a justfile recipe, pre-commit hook, or CI step? What's the total indirect usage across all charly/sk projects?

## Method

1. Search all projects for justfile recipes that call `pretender` (already done: 5 projects)
2. Search for pre-commit hooks (lefthook, prek, .git/hooks) that call pretender (already done: 2 projects)
3. Search for CI workflows (.github/workflows/) that call pretender
4. Search for `pretender.toml` files (already done: 4 projects)
5. For each project, check pi session logs for `just complexity` or `just check` calls that might trigger pretender indirectly

## Deliverable

A report section for the agent-adoption research document with the indirect usage numbers.

## Acceptance Criteria

- All indirect usage paths are enumerated
- A corrected total usage number is produced (direct + indirect)
- The report is added to the research document

title: Add pretender to wai way code-quality guide topic
description:
## Problem

`wai way` has topic guides for "ci", "hooks", "coverage", "docs", "devxp", "ai", "gh", "issues", "specs" — but no "code-quality" topic. pretender has no discoverability path through wai way's interactive guides.

## Expected

Add a `wai way code-quality` topic guide that walks agents through:

1. What pretender does (structural code quality checks across multiple languages)
2. How to install: `cargo install pretender`
3. How to initialize: `pretender init --non-interactive`
4. How to run: `pretender check .`
5. How to integrate: add to justfile, CI, pre-commit hooks
6. Metric thresholds and how to configure them

## Format

Follow the existing pattern in `wai/src/commands/way/mod.rs` (e.g., `guide_ci()`, `guide_hooks()`). The guide is a facilitation template that instructs an LLM to lead an interactive discussion.

## Acceptance Criteria

- `wai way code-quality` prints a guide that covers the 6 topics above
- `wai way --list` (or equivalent) shows "code-quality" in available topics
- Format matches existing topic guides
close_reason: Already implemented as part of pretender-65j (check_pretender() + guide_code_quality() were added together)

title: Publish research: why agents don't use pretender frequently
description:
## Problem

The research investigation into "why agents don't use pretender frequently" (conducted 2026-07-17) is complete but needs revision before publication. The Rule of 5 review identified 12 issues, including 3 HIGH findings.

## Deliverable

Publish the corrected research document. Target location: `docs/research/agent-adoption.md` or similar.

## Prerequisites

Before publishing, address these findings:

1. **CORR-001** — Split the invocation count: 55 total (41 from pretender's own development, 14 from other projects). The headline should use 14, not 55.
2. **EDGE-001** — Add a section acknowledging the assumption that increased adoption is the goal. Note the user may be intentionally deferring.
3. **CLAR-001** — Add a 2-3 sentence explanation of how wai way works (check_*() functions).
4. **CLAR-002** — Add counts to CLI friction examples (e.g., "3 instances of --threshold misuse").
5. **EDGE-002** — Check indirect usage through justfile wrappers (just complexity, just check).
6. **EXCL-001** — Add a prioritization matrix for recommendations.
7. **EXCL-002** — Add success metrics (target: 30+ invocations, 8+ projects with .pretender/).

## Acceptance Criteria

- All 7 corrections above are applied
- Document is saved to `docs/research/agent-adoption.md`
- Peer review before publishing

title: Create pretender project template for CLAUDE.md/AGENTS.md
description:
## Problem

Only 1 project (cositos) mentions pretender in its CLAUDE.md/AGENTS.md. Agents have no discoverability path to learn that pretender is available for code quality checks. The tool is invisible unless the agent already knows about it.

## Expected

Create a template snippet that projects can include in their CLAUDE.md or AGENTS.md:

```markdown
## Code Quality

- Run `pretender check .` for structural code quality checks
- Run `pretender complexity <path>` to see cyclomatic complexity
- Configuration: `pretender.toml` at repo root
- Install: `cargo install pretender`
```

Where should this template live?
- Option A: In `pretender/docs/` as a reference file
- Option B: In `wai`'s agent-config/resources as a standard projection
- Option C: In the `llms.txt` or `llm.txt` of the pretender repo

## Acceptance Criteria

- Template snippet is created and documented
- At least 2 projects add the snippet to their CLAUDE.md/AGENTS.md
- The template is discoverable via wai sync or similar

title: Research: add success metrics for pretender adoption
description:
## Problem

The adoption research doesn't define what "success" looks like. Without measurable targets, we can't tell if fixes are working.

## Deliverable

Define measurable success metrics for pretender adoption. These should be added to the research document at `docs/research/agent-adoption.md`.

## Proposed Metrics

- **Target**: Non-pretender project invocations reach 30+ within 2 months (up from 14)
- **Target**: 8+ projects have `.pretender/` config (up from 4)
- **Target**: 10+ projects mention pretender in justfile (up from 5)
- **Target**: 5+ projects mention pretender in CLAUDE.md/AGENTS.md (up from 1)
- **Target**: `wai way` shows pretender check as "pass" for all charly projects

## Acceptance Criteria

- Metrics are defined, documented, and reviewed
- A baseline measurement is recorded
- A review date is set (e.g., 2 months from now)

title: F1: pretender — adopt shared JSON envelope crate
description:
Part of the F1 epic: Extract shared JSON envelope crate from dont and adopt suite-wide.

Scope for pretender: Replace bare JSON array output (e.g., pretender doctor --format json returns an array) with the shared envelope. Ensure all --format json responses are wrapped in {ok, envelope_version, cli_version, envelope_kind, data, warnings, hints, meta}.

Files: pretender/ -- after shared crate is released, add it as a dependency and wrap all --format json output.

Dependencies: Blocked on dont-2j6o (the F1 epic in dont -- the crate must exist first).

title: F19: pretender — standardize coverage tooling (taiki-e/install-action, codecov upload, match dont pattern)
description:
Source: CLI consistency audit F19

Problem: pretender's coverage job uses "cargo install cargo-tarpaulin --locked" (compiles from source every run, slow) and uploads only to artifacts (no Codecov reporting). The suite standard is "taiki-e/install-action@cargo-tarpaulin" (binary-cached, fast) + "codecov/codecov-action@v5" with "cargo tarpaulin --out xml --output-dir coverage".

Expected: Switch to taiki-e/install-action for tarpaulin install, add codecov upload step, and use the standard output flags.

Files: pretender/.github/workflows/ci.yml — coverage job section.

Reference: dont/.github/workflows/ci.yml is the canonical template for coverage.

Verification: "grep 'taiki-e/install-action@cargo-tarpaulin' pretender/.github/workflows/ci.yml" should match.

Classification: AFK (agent-friendly).

title: F20: pretender — add --all-targets to clippy in CI (currently misses tests/benches)
description:
Source: CLI consistency audit F20

Problem: pretender's CI runs "cargo clippy -- -D warnings" (lib/bin targets only), while testaruda's CI runs "cargo clippy --all-targets -- -D warnings" (also lints tests, examples, benches). The stricter scope should be applied suite-wide.

Expected: Change CI to "cargo clippy --all-targets -- -D warnings" so tests and examples are also linted.

Files: pretender/.github/workflows/ci.yml — add "--all-targets" to the clippy step.

Verification: "grep 'clippy' pretender/.github/workflows/ci.yml | grep 'all-targets'" should match.

Classification: AFK (agent-friendly).

title: Upgrade tree-sitter to 0.25+ (unblocks Clojure plugin)
description:
## Why
The tree-sitter-clojure crate requires tree-sitter >= 0.25.6, but the project
is currently pinned to 0.23.x via the main `tree-sitter` crate.

Upgrading tree-sitter to 0.25+ will also unblock newer versions of other
grammar crates (e.g., tree-sitter-c-sharp 0.23.5+).

## Scope
- Update `tree-sitter` version in pretender/Cargo.toml (currently "0.23")
- Adapt any API changes in `pretender/src/engine.rs` (`QueryCursor::matches`
  API changed in 0.24+ — no longer implements Iterator directly)
- Update all grammar crate versions that have newer releases
- Verify all 109+ unit tests and 76+ CLI tests still pass
- Test rust, python, javascript, typescript, go, java, c, cpp, ruby, r, julia fixtures

## Blocked issues
- pretender-i9o (Clojure plugin) depends on this

## Acceptance
- `cargo build` succeeds
- All existing tests pass
- tree-sitter-clojure can be added without conflicts

title: feat: add R language plugin (tree-sitter-r)
description:
## Why
cositos has an R backend that pretender cannot parse. `pretender check r/` returns `{"files":[]}` with exit 0. R is the dominant language for statistical computing and is widely used in data-science adjacent projects.

## Crate
`tree-sitter-r = "1.3.0"` is available on crates.io. Note: this is on a 1.x version line (not 0.23); check API compatibility with the current tree-sitter 0.23 integration layer before pinning.

## What to build
Following the exact pattern of existing language plugins:

1. Add `tree-sitter-r` to `pretender/Cargo.toml` (verify 0.23 API compat; pin to a compatible version).
2. Create `languages/r/plugin.toml` — extensions `[".r", ".R"]`, tree_sitter source, branch/assertion/smell_weights tables.
3. Create `languages/r/metrics.scm` — tree-sitter queries capturing:
   - Function definitions: `function_definition` (R uses `name <- function(...) {...}` — the name is typically an assignment LHS, not part of the function node; capture `function_definition` and walk up to the enclosing assignment for the name)
   - Branches: `if_statement`, `for_statement`, `while_statement`, `tryCatch` call detection
   - Calls and assignments for ABC metric
4. Register the plugin in the builtin registry.
5. Add a test fixture `tests/fixtures/r/` with a sample `.R` file and a detection test.

## Note on R grammar complexity
R's grammar is unusual: functions are anonymous by default and typically assigned. The `metrics.scm` may need to handle the `left_assignment` / `right_assignment` node wrapping a `function_definition`. Review how tree-sitter-r models this before writing queries.

## Acceptance
`pretender check tests/fixtures/r/` parses at least one function, reports metrics, and exits 0.
`pretender check` on a directory containing only `.R` files does not return `{"files":[]}`.
CI passes.

## Related
- pretender-4uh (F19: silent exit 0 on unsupported paths)

title: feat: add C# language plugin (tree-sitter-c-sharp)
description:
## Why
cositos has a C# backend that pretender cannot parse. `pretender check csharp/` returns `{"files":[]}` with exit 0. C# is one of the most widely used languages and a first-class target for complexity gating.

## Crate
`tree-sitter-c-sharp = "0.23.5"` is available on crates.io and tracks the 0.23 API.

## What to build
Following the exact pattern of existing language plugins (e.g. `languages/java/`):

1. Add `tree-sitter-c-sharp` to `pretender/Cargo.toml` dependencies.
2. Create `languages/csharp/plugin.toml` — extensions `[".cs"]`, `tree_sitter = { source = "builtin:tree-sitter-c-sharp", rev = "0.23" }`, branch/assertion/smell_weights tables.
3. Create `languages/csharp/metrics.scm` — tree-sitter queries capturing:
   - Function definitions: `method_declaration`, `constructor_declaration`, `local_function_statement`
   - Branches: `if_statement`, `else_clause`, `for_statement`, `foreach_statement`, `while_statement`, `do_statement`, `catch_clause`, `conditional_expression`, `switch_section`, `&&`/`||`
   - Calls and assignments for ABC metric
   - Assertions: `Assert.*` method calls
4. Register the plugin in the builtin registry.
5. Add a test fixture `tests/fixtures/csharp/` with a sample `.cs` file and a detection test.

## Acceptance
`pretender check tests/fixtures/csharp/` parses at least one method, reports metrics, and exits 0.
`pretender check` on a directory containing only `.cs` files does not return `{"files":[]}`.
CI passes.

## Related
- pretender-4uh (F19: silent exit 0 on unsupported paths)

title: feat: add Julia language plugin (tree-sitter-julia)
description:
## Why
cositos has a Julia backend (`julia/src/`) that pretender cannot parse. `pretender check julia/src` returns `{"files":[]}` with exit 0 — silent false confidence. Julia is a common scientific computing language and a realistic target for complexity gating.

## Crate
`tree-sitter-julia = "0.23.1"` is available on crates.io and tracks the same 0.23 API as all current builtin grammars.

## What to build
Following the exact pattern of existing language plugins (e.g. `languages/python/`):

1. Add `tree-sitter-julia` to `pretender/Cargo.toml` dependencies.
2. Create `languages/julia/plugin.toml` — extensions `[".jl"]`, `tree_sitter = { source = "builtin:tree-sitter-julia", rev = "0.23" }`, branch/assertion/smell_weights tables.
3. Create `languages/julia/metrics.scm` — tree-sitter queries capturing:
   - Function definitions: `function_definition`, `short_function_definition`
   - Branches: `if_statement`, `elseif_clause`, `for_statement`, `while_statement`, `try_statement`, `ternary_expression`, `&&`/`||` operators
   - Calls and assignments for ABC metric
4. Register the plugin in the builtin registry (wherever Python/Rust etc. are registered in `src/`).
5. Add a test fixture `tests/fixtures/julia/` with a sample `.jl` file and a test asserting at least one function is detected.

## Acceptance
`pretender check tests/fixtures/julia/` parses at least one function, reports metrics, and exits 0.
`pretender check` on a directory containing only `.jl` files does not return `{"files":[]}`.
CI passes with the new plugin included.

## Related
- pretender-4uh (F19: silent exit 0 on unsupported paths — companion fix; this removes the need for the workaround on Julia paths)

title: [bug] F19: pretender exits 0 with empty results on paths containing no supported-language files
description:
## Finding
From cositos dogfooding evaluation (sandbox/charly-vibes-tools/dev-tooling-evaluation.md §F19).

`pretender check <path>` exits 0 and returns `{"files":[]}` when given a directory containing only unsupported languages (e.g. Julia, C#, R, Clojure). No warning, no error, just silent empty success.

## Reproduction
```
# cositos project added Julia, C#, R, Clojure backends after initial Python+JS setup
pretender check julia/src csharp r clojure/src
→ {"files": []}   # exit 0
```

## Impact
The complexity gate appears comprehensive (it runs, exits 0) but it only covers the original languages. Every new backend added in a language pretender can't parse is silently unchecked forever. This gives *false confidence* — the gate looks green but it only covers a fraction of the codebase.

In cositos this was worked around with a manual coverage-manifest gate (a checked-in file listing every backend directory with its declared quality-tool binding; a build step fails if a directory is undeclared or claims pretender coverage but pretender parses zero files). But the root fix belongs in pretender.

## Supported languages (for reference)
C, C++, Go, Java, JavaScript, Python, Ruby, Rust, TypeScript.

## Suggested fix
When explicit paths are supplied and zero files match any supported grammar, exit non-zero (or at minimum emit a loud warning to stderr) with a message like:
```
warning: no files matched a supported grammar in: julia/src, csharp, r, clojure/src
```
This turns "silently uncovered" into a caught misconfiguration.
acceptance_criteria:
`pretender check <dir>` exits non-zero (or emits a stderr warning) when the given paths produce zero parsed files and no supported language grammar matched.
`pretender check <supported-lang-dir>` (e.g. a Python or JS tree) is unaffected.
A test fixture in `tests/` covers the empty-result path (a directory containing only an unsupported-language file) and asserts the non-zero exit or warning output.
notes: Likely change site: the file-walker / grammar-dispatch logic in pretender check (src/check.rs or equivalent). The guard should only fire when explicit paths are supplied and zero files match — not when a path simply happens to have no violations.

title: F6: pretender --version is rejected; inconsistent with wai, dont, ah
description:
## Parent
Evaluation report: ~/para/projects/astra-zeneca/dev/sandbox/charly-vibes-tools/dev-tooling-evaluation.md §F6

## What to build

`pretender --version` exits with an error:
```
error: unexpected argument '--version' found
```

All other tools in the suite (`wai`, `dont`, `ah`) accept `--version` and print the installed version. This inconsistency is especially noticeable because the suite is otherwise well-coordinated.

## Files / Systems
- `pretender/pretender/src/main.rs`, `Cli` struct at L40–45
- The `#[command(name = "pretender")]` annotation — add `.version(env!("CARGO_PKG_VERSION"))` via clap's derive macro

## Fix

Add the `version` attribute to the top-level `Cli` struct:
```rust
#[derive(Parser)]
#[command(name = "pretender", version)]
struct Cli { … }
```

Clap's `version` attribute reads `CARGO_PKG_VERSION` automatically.

## Acceptance Criteria
- [ ] `pretender --version` exits 0 and prints the crate version (e.g. `pretender 0.1.0`)
- [ ] `pretender -V` also works (clap short flag)
- [ ] No other subcommand behaviour changes

---
**CRITICAL: Follow Test Driven Development and Tidy First workflows.**
- Write tests *before* writing implementation code.
- Clean up related code *before* adding new functionality.
acceptance_criteria: pretender --version exits 0 and prints the crate version, consistent with wai/dont/ah

title: no warning when running check without pretender.toml
description: Running `pretender check` without a config file silently uses default thresholds with no warning. Users who haven't run `pretender init` get results against defaults they may not have chosen. Should print 'no pretender.toml found, using defaults — run pretender init to configure' to stderr.
notes: Verify: delete pretender.toml (or run in a temp dir), then run `pretender check <file> 2>&1` and confirm the stderr output contains a line about using defaults and suggesting `pretender init`.

title: check shows ✗ in guidance/tiered mode even though it exits 0
description:
In `guidance` and `tiered` modes, `pretender check` exits with code 0 (success) even when violations exist, but the human output still marks files with ✗ and says VIOLATION. The visual signal contradicts the exit code semantics.

Fix: use mode-aware indicators in the human output:
- `guidance` → ⚠ advisory (exits 0)
- `tiered` → ⚠ advisory (exits 0)
- `gate` → ✗ blocking (exits 1 on violations)

Only gate mode should use ✗ and the word VIOLATION. Guidance and tiered should use ⚠ and say ADVISORY.

Verify: `pretender check pretender/src/config.rs --mode guidance` shows ⚠ and exits 0. `pretender check pretender/src/config.rs --mode gate` shows ✗ and exits 1.

title: check --format junit/markdown listed as valid but not implemented
description: The `check` command advertises `junit` and `markdown` in its `--format` possible values, but both immediately fail with 'not yet implemented'. Listing unavailable options in the help creates false expectations and breaks scripts. Either remove them from the enum until implemented, or document them as experimental/planned.
notes:
When junit and markdown formats are eventually implemented, re-add the enum variants and remove the not_implemented stub. Tracked under pretender-t2m.

Verify: `pretender check pretender/src/main.rs --format junit` exits with a clear error ('unsupported format') or the variant is absent from `--help` output. Neither 'not yet implemented (tracked: ...)' nor a silent exit 2 is acceptable.

title: check --staged produces no output when no files are staged
description: Running `pretender check . --staged` when there are no staged files exits 0 with no output. Users see nothing and may think the check ran and passed. Should output a message like 'No staged files to check.' to make the no-op explicit.
notes: Verify: run `git stash` to ensure no staged files, then `pretender check . --staged`. Output should contain 'No staged files to check.' and exit 0.

title: complexity output shows raw scores with no threshold context
description: `pretender complexity` outputs bare numbers like 'write_human_report: 11' with no indication of what's acceptable. Users can't tell if 11 is a problem without separately running `pretender explain cyclomatic`. The output should include the configured threshold, similar to how `check` shows 'VIOLATION cyclomatic 11 > 10'.
notes:
Show the configured cyclomatic threshold next to each score, matching the format of `check` output. E.g. 'write_human_report: 11 (threshold: 10) ✗'.

Verify: `pretender complexity pretender/src/main.rs` output includes threshold values next to each score. Scores at or below threshold show no marker; scores above show a violation indicator.

title: complexity only accepts one path, inconsistent with other commands
description: The `pretender complexity` command only accepts a single file path, while `check`, `duplication`, and `mutation` all accept multiple paths. This inconsistency makes scripting harder and surprises users who expect the same interface across commands.
notes:
Output format for multiple paths: one sorted list across all files, same as `check` (per-file header then per-function scores). No merging of results across files.

Verify: `pretender complexity pretender/src/main.rs pretender/src/config.rs` outputs scores for functions from both files without error.

title: init --non-interactive and --defaults are duplicate flags with identical behavior
description: Both `pretender init --non-interactive` and `pretender init --defaults` do exactly the same thing (skip prompts, use defaults) per the help text and implementation. Having two flags with the same behavior is confusing. One should be removed or made an alias for the other.
notes:
Keep `--non-interactive` (the conventional name used by most CLIs). Remove `--defaults`.

Verify: `pretender init --defaults` should fail with 'unexpected argument'. `pretender init --non-interactive` should still work and skip prompts.

title: check output is too noisy: shows all functions, not just violations
description:
Running `pretender check` on a single file produces 78+ lines because every function is listed with all metrics, even when passing. Violations are hard to spot — they're buried between passing functions.

Fix: change the default human output to show only violating functions. Add a `--verbose` flag to restore the current full per-function metrics view.

Verify: `pretender check pretender/src/main.rs` output contains only VIOLATION lines and file-level summary (< 20 lines). `pretender check pretender/src/main.rs --verbose` restores the current full output.

title: Fix stale beads preflight checks for Rust workspace
description:
## Why
`bd preflight` currently suggests Go-specific checks (`go test`, `golangci-lint`, `gofmt`) even though this repository is a Rust workspace. That makes the cleanup/close workflow misleading.

## What
- update beads preflight/project metadata so Rust quality gates are suggested
- ensure the recommended checks match `just ci` / cargo-based verification

## Acceptance Criteria
- `bd preflight` no longer suggests Go-specific commands for this repo
- recommended quality gates match the Rust workspace
notes: Root cause: bd preflight is currently hardcoded upstream and does not honor repo-local config for language-specific quality gates. Local workaround landed in this repo: `just preflight` now aliases the Rust verification pipeline (`just ci`), and README points contributors there instead of bd preflight until beads supports repo-aware customization.
close_reason: workaround landed: just preflight aliases just ci; upstream beads fix is out of scope for this project

title: Implement tracer-bullet feedback loop for recurring cognitive_max violations
description:
Implement the first end-to-end recurring-violation feedback loop described in pretender/docs/feedback-loop-design.md. Focus on a single tracer-bullet signal: cognitive_max.

## Why
Pretender already computes and reports structural findings, but those findings disappear after each run. A small tracer-bullet implementation will prove the persistence, aggregation, and feedback architecture before generalizing to the full rule set.

## Scope
- Emit ViolationEvent records for cognitive_max findings from pretender check
- Persist events under .pretender/history/events.jsonl
- Compute a recurring-hotspot summary using the design thresholds
- Expose the summary in a simple machine-readable and/or markdown-friendly form
- Add tests for recurrence thresholds and retention pruning

## References
- pretender/docs/feedback-loop-design.md
- closed design issue: pretender-5rk

## Non-Goals
- Full multi-metric generalization
- Automatic config/spec/prompt edits
- Cloud or cross-repo storage

title: fix: remove broad #![allow(dead_code)] from metrics.rs, roles.rs, and config.rs
description:
The crate has a top-level #![allow(dead_code)] in lib.rs or main.rs that silences all dead-code warnings across the entire codebase. This masks the sequence_id dedup arm in metrics.rs and potentially other dead code introduced by the abc/exported-threshold gaps.

Fix: remove the blanket allow and triage each warning individually.
Expected warning locations after removal:
- metrics.rs: sequence_id dedup arm (Some(id) branch) — this is dead by design until CORR-001 is fixed; add a targeted #[allow] with a comment referencing that issue
- roles.rs: any exported_* fields not yet checked — keep as-is until pretender-2rd is fixed
- config.rs: abc_max field if added before pretender-xgz is complete

Acceptance: cargo check (or cargo clippy) produces zero warnings after fix, or every remaining allow is targeted (not blanket) with a comment explaining why.

title: fix: walk_block() and collect_nested_blocks() in engine.rs duplicate the same traversal logic
description:
walk_block() and collect_nested_blocks() in engine.rs share traversal logic that could be unified into a single recursive block-walker helper, reducing duplication and making future traversal changes apply in one place.

Scope: refactor only — no behavior change. The two functions serve different call sites; the shared logic is the recursive child-node walk. Extract a shared private helper that both can delegate to.

Acceptance:
- All existing tests pass unchanged (no test modifications needed or permitted)
- The extracted helper has a clear type signature and is tested indirectly through the existing call sites
- Clippy passes with no new warnings

title: Apply formal-verification readiness fixes to specs
description: Per formal-verification-evaluator findings: add invariants, atomicity claims, liveness guarantees, and DbC pre/postconditions to cache, plugin-trust, baseline, and watch specs. Closes the NEEDS_FORMALIZATION_WORK gap surfaced in the eval.
close_reason: Applied 9 formal-verification fixes across cache/plugin-trust/baseline/watch specs; all 4 proposals validate strict; commit 435d762 pushed to origin/main

title: Fix all testability/implementability findings from spec eval
description: Apply fixes from testability-implementability-evaluator report: 1) Remove unfalsifiable WASM placeholder requirement; 2) Replace ambiguous 'silently' with verifiable behavior in baseline+watch specs; 3) Address other phrase-level transformations identified in the eval.
close_reason: Spec testability fixes applied across baseline, watch, plugin-trust, and suppression proposals; all four pass openspec validate --strict. Committed in f527149 and pushed.

title: Rule-of-5 review fixes: branch cleanup + mode wiring + ABC captures + CLI stubs
description: Address tractable gaps from the rule-of-5-universal review: DRAFT-001 (stub missing CLI commands), DRAFT-002 (wire guidance/tiered/gate modes into exit code), DRAFT-004 (emit Node::Call/Node::Assignment so ABC isn't always 0), DRAFT-006 (add sarif/junit/markdown format variants as NotImplemented), DRAFT-007 (drop unused BranchKind::NullCoalesce/EarlyReturn). Plugin loader (DRAFT-003), --staged/--diff-only (DRAFT-006 deeper), full feature impls remain in their existing beads tickets.
close_reason: Tractable review fixes landed: dropped unused BranchKind variants; wired guidance/tiered/gate modes into check exit code; engine now emits Node::Call + Node::Assignment so ABC reflects real captures; stubbed init/report/duplication/mutation/hooks/ci/plugins/explain (exit 2 'not yet implemented' with bd tracker); added Sarif/Junit/Markdown format variants gated to pretender-t2m; --staged/--diff-only gated to pretender-a80. 50 tests, clippy clean, fmt clean.

title: Explore feedback loop: track recurring structural violations to surface as constraints
description:
When pretender catches structural violations (complexity, duplication, nesting depth), nothing persists this signal beyond the current session. This ticket explores how to track recurring violations and feed them back into the development process.

**Note:** pretender currently calculates metrics (cyclomatic complexity, cognitive complexity, nesting, ABC) but does not yet compare them against thresholds to emit violations. This ticket covers both: (1) designing the violation emission path, and (2) persisting and aggregating those signals.

## Deliverable

A design document at `pretender/docs/feedback-loop-design.md` answering the questions below, reviewed and approved. Followed by an implementation ticket.

## Starting points

- `pretender/src/config.rs:166-260` — threshold definitions (AppThresholds, TestThresholds, etc.)
- `pretender/src/metrics.rs:6-45` — metric calculations (cyclomatic, cognitive, nesting, ABC)
- `pretender/src/main.rs:44-73` — main entry: calculates metrics but doesn't compare to thresholds
- `pretender/src/roles.rs:171-231` — EffectiveThresholds applied per role

## Questions to answer

- What violation taxonomy to use? (pretender's existing threshold categories vs. something richer)
- How to aggregate: per project, per agent model, per code area?
- Should recurring violations auto-generate openspec constraints or pretender profile defaults?
- What threshold: how many occurrences before a pattern is "recurring"?

## Integration points

- openspec specs: surface recurring patterns as constraints in future proposals
- pretender profiles: auto-adjust thresholds based on project-specific violation history
- Agent prompts: inject "known weak spots" into system prompts for specific projects

## Sequencing

This is a generalization ticket. Start after the espectacular tracer-bullet proves the end-to-end feedback loop with one concrete signal.

title: CLI: pretender report command (human/markdown/html)
description: pretender report: read last run output and generate formatted report. Flags: --format human|markdown|html (default: human), --output <path> (for markdown/html). human: re-renders terminal color output from last run. markdown: suitable for GITHUB_STEP_SUMMARY and PR comments. html: self-contained file for sharing offline. Reads from the last JSON output written by pretender check. Verify: pretender check --format=json --output=/tmp/last.json && pretender report --format=markdown produces non-empty markdown with function names and scores.

title: 5 additional languages: Go, Java, Ruby, C, C++
description: Write .scm query files and plugin.toml for Go, Java, Ruby, C, C++. Each file ~30-80 lines capturing function definitions and branch types. Go: function_declaration, method_declaration, if_statement, for_statement, type_switch_statement, binary_expression (&&/||). Java/Ruby/C/C++: equivalent constructs. No engine code changes needed — pure .scm authoring.
notes: Each language plugin must produce correct CodeUnit output when run against a fixture file. Add tests/fixtures/<lang>_sample.<ext> for each language (Go, Java, Ruby, C, C++) with at least 2 functions of known complexity. Verify: cargo test -p pretender-engine parse_<lang>_fixture green for each language. Java/Ruby/C/C++ captures needed: function/method definitions, if/else, loops, logical operators — see Python and Go .scm files as reference.
close_reason: Added Go, Java, Ruby, C, C++ plugins with .scm queries, plugin.toml manifests, fixture files, and CLI tests. All 71 tests pass.

title: GitHub Actions CI generator
description: pretender ci generate github: write .github/workflows/pretender.yml. Workflow: on PR and main push, checkout with fetch-depth:0, pretender-tool/setup@v1 action, pretender check --diff-base=origin/main --format=sarif --output=pretender.sarif, github/codeql-action/upload-sarif@v3, on failure: pretender report --format=markdown >> $GITHUB_STEP_SUMMARY.
notes: pretender-tool/setup@v1 is a placeholder — it must be published as a separate GitHub Action before the generated workflow is functional end-to-end. Generate the YAML verbatim per the spec, but document in a comment at the top of the generated file: # Note: pretender-tool/setup@v1 must be published before this workflow is functional. No issue for the setup action is tracked in this repo; track separately when approaching V1 release.

title: SARIF 2.1.0 output format
description: Emit valid SARIF 2.1.0 using serde_sarif. Schema: runs[0].tool.driver with rules array (one per metric), results array with locations (physicalLocation.artifactLocation.uri + region.startLine). Integrate with pretender check --format sarif --output pretender.sarif. Test against GitHub Code Scanning sarif schema validator.
notes: Verify SARIF validity with: npx @microsoft/sarif-multitool validate pretender.sarif (or offline at https://sarifweb.azurewebsites.net). The GitHub Code Scanning ingest requires valid schema — invalid SARIF silently drops all findings. Verify: pretender check --format=sarif --output=pretender.sarif && npx @microsoft/sarif-multitool validate pretender.sarif exits 0.

title: Three operating modes: guidance / tiered / gate
description: Wire the mode setting into check command output and exit code. guidance: always exit 0, print informational report. tiered: exit 0, color-code green/yellow/red per [bands] config, highlight yellow violations. gate: exit non-zero when any metric exceeds *_max threshold. Output format: '✓ src/parser.py — all green', '⚠ src/runner.py — run_pipeline(): cognitive 22 (yellow)', '✗ src/legacy/dispatch.py — dispatch_event(): cyclomatic 18 (red)'.
notes: Week 1 default: pretender check runs in guidance-mode behavior before this ticket lands (always exit 0, informational output). This ticket wires the mode setting from pretender.toml into the output and exit code. Do not attempt to read mode from config in pretender-81c — stub as guidance until 6aw closes. Verify: with mode=gate in pretender.toml, pretender check exits non-zero when any metric exceeds *_max. With mode=guidance, always exits 0 regardless of findings.

title: min_assertions metric for test role
description: Detect test files (role=test), count assertion captures per CodeUnit using @assert.* capture patterns defined in plugin.toml. Flag any test function with assertion count < min_assertions (default 1). Python patterns: assert, self.assert*, pytest.raises, pytest.warns. Go: t.Error*, t.Fatal*, require.*, assert.*. Rust: assert!, assert_eq!, assert_ne!.
close_reason: Implemented per-CodeUnit assertion counting, enforced min_assertions for test role, expanded Python assertion captures, and added CLI/parser regressions.

title: Cognitive complexity metric
description: Implement cognitive complexity: sum of branch_weight * (1 + nesting_at) for all branches. Use SonarSource published weights as defaults, user-overridable per-language in plugin.toml. Threshold: ≤15 app, ≤5 test. Wire into pretender check output.
close_reason: Implemented plugin-driven cognitive branch weights, added parser/metric regressions, and verified cognitive violations in check output.

title: ABC scoring with smell weights (pretender complexity)
description: Implement ABC metric: A=assignments, B=branches (smell-weighted), C=calls (smell-weighted), score=sqrt(A^2+B^2+C^2). Add per-language smell weights in plugin.toml. pretender complexity command: scan files, sort CodeUnits worst-first by ABC score, emit top N (default 10). Python weights: eval=5, exec=5, compile=3. Go: reflect.*=3, unsafe.*=4. Rust: unsafe=4, transmute=5, macro_rules!=1.5.

title: Rust language plugin (.scm + plugin.toml)
description: Write languages/rust/metrics.scm capturing: fn_item, closure_expression, if_expression, match_arm, loop_expression, while_expression, for_expression. Smell weights: unsafe=4.0, std::mem::transmute=5.0, macro_rules!=1.5. Assertion patterns: assert!, assert_eq!, assert_ne!, panic!.

title: F9: pretender — document <PATHS> positional argument in 'pretender check --help' (blank description)
description:
Source: CLI consistency audit F9

Problem: "pretender check --help" shows "<PATHS>..." with a blank description — the only undocumented argument surface in an otherwise fully-annotated pretender CLI.

Expected: Add a doc-comment to the PATHS positional argument in pretender's clap definition, e.g. "Paths to files or directories to analyze for complexity metrics."

Files: pretender/src/... (the clap command definition for the "check" subcommand) — add a doc-comment or about string to the PATHS argument.

Verification: "pretender check --help | grep -A1 PATHS" should show a description.

Classification: AFK (agent-friendly).

title: feat: add Clojure language plugin (tree-sitter-clojure)
description:
## Why
cositos has a Clojure backend that pretender cannot parse. `pretender check clojure/src` returns `{"files":[]}` with exit 0. Clojure's functional style means traditional cyclomatic complexity is less meaningful, but nesting depth, parameter count, and function length are still useful signals.

## Crate
`tree-sitter-clojure = "0.1.0"` is available on crates.io. Note: this is an early 0.x release; verify tree-sitter API compatibility before pinning.

## What to build
Following the existing plugin pattern:

1. Add `tree-sitter-clojure` to `pretender/Cargo.toml` (verify compat).
2. Create `languages/clojure/plugin.toml` — extensions `[".clj", ".cljs", ".cljc", ".edn"]`, tree_sitter source, branch/assertion/smell_weights tables.
3. Create `languages/clojure/metrics.scm` — queries capturing:
   - Function definitions: `defn`, `defn-`, `fn` forms (list nodes with `defn`/`fn` as first symbol)
   - Branches: `if`, `when`, `cond`, `case`, `try`/`catch` forms
   - Nesting depth is particularly valuable for Clojure (deeply nested `let`/threading macros are a common smell)
4. Register the plugin in the builtin registry.
5. Add a test fixture `tests/fixtures/clojure/` with a sample `.clj` file and a detection test.

## Note on Clojure grammar complexity
Clojure's homoiconic syntax means all forms are lists; tree-sitter-clojure represents them as `list_lit` nodes. Function definitions and branches are identified by the first element being a specific symbol. This is structurally different from the C-family grammars — the `.scm` queries will need to match on symbol text, not node type.

Priority lowered to P3 because Clojure has smaller practical overlap with the cositos workflow compared to Julia/C#/R, and the grammar integration is more complex.

## Acceptance
`pretender check tests/fixtures/clojure/` parses at least one function definition, reports metrics, and exits 0.
`pretender check` on a directory containing only `.clj` files does not return `{"files":[]}`.
CI passes.

## Related
- pretender-4uh (F19: silent exit 0 on unsupported paths)

title: duplication --min-nodes and --cross-file flags have no help text
description: In `pretender duplication --help`, the flags `--min-nodes` and `--cross-file` appear with no description. Users can't understand what they control without reading source or documentation. Should add descriptions like: '--min-nodes: minimum AST node count to consider a clone (default: 10)' and '--cross-file: detect clones that span multiple files'.
notes: Verify: `pretender duplication --help` output shows descriptions for both `--min-nodes` and `--cross-file` flags. Descriptions should explain what they control and mention the default for `--min-nodes` (10).

title: ABC metric displays excessive decimal places in human output
description: In `check` human output, ABC violations are displayed as 'abc 52.16320542297991 > 30' — a long floating-point number that is hard to read. The human output should round to 2 decimal places (e.g. 'abc 52.16 > 30'). JSON output can keep full precision.
notes:
Apply rounding to 2 decimal places in the human output formatter only. JSON output retains full float precision.

Verify: `pretender check pretender/src/main.rs --format human` shows ABC values like '52.16' not '52.16320542297991'. `pretender check pretender/src/main.rs --format json` retains full precision.

title: report command description says 'Pretty TUI' but there is no TUI
description: The help text for `pretender report` reads 'Pretty TUI or HTML report from the last check run'. There is no TUI — the command outputs plain human-readable text, markdown, or HTML. The description misleads users expecting an interactive terminal UI.
notes:
Replace 'Pretty TUI or HTML report' with 'Human-readable, markdown, or HTML report'. One-line doc string change in the clap Subcommand derive.

Verify: `pretender report --help` description does not contain 'TUI'.

title: Write getting-started guide
description:
New users need a narrative entry point before hitting the reference docs. The four existing doc tickets are all reference material; there is no quickstart.

## Location
Place at docs/getting-started.md. Make it the first link in the root README under a new 'Documentation' section.

## Content (end-to-end workflow)
1. Install: cargo install pretender
2. Initialise: pretender init (what it writes and why)
3. First check: pretender check . (what the output means, how to read severity bands)
4. Fix a violation: walkthrough of a cyclomatic violation → refactor → recheck
5. Pre-commit hook: pretender hooks install (what it does, how to uninstall)
6. CI integration: pretender ci generate github (brief; link to docs/mutation.md for mutation gate)

## Verification
A developer unfamiliar with the project can follow the guide from zero to a passing pretender check in a fresh repo without consulting other docs.

title: Document mutation testing integration and supported tools
description:
pretender mutation wraps four tools (mutmut for Python, cargo-mutants for Rust, Stryker for JS/TS, PIT for Java) but this is nowhere documented.

## Location
Place at docs/mutation.md. Add a 'Mutation testing' link in the root README.

## Content to cover
- Tool selection table: language → tool (mutmut, cargo-mutants, Stryker, PIT)
- Prerequisites: each tool must be installed separately; list install commands per tool
- What --dry-run shows: lists planned mutation sites without running tests
  Example: pretender mutation . --dry-run
- How --score-min gates CI: exits non-zero if mutation score < threshold
  Example: pretender mutation . --score-min 80
- JSON output format (prose description of MutationReport fields: score_pct, killed, total, survived, tool, survivors list with file/line/description/missed_tests)
  Example: pretender mutation . --format json
- Cross-reference: link to the GitHub Actions workflow generated by 'pretender ci generate github' as the recommended CI integration pattern

## Verification
All four tools are mentioned, all flags are documented with example commands, and the JSON field descriptions match the MutationReport struct in mutation.rs.

title: Add pretender.toml reference documentation
description:
The README has only a minimal config snippet. Need a full configuration reference.

## Location
Place at docs/configuration.md. Add a 'Configuration reference' link in the root README.

## Sections to cover
Every key in every table with its type, default value, and a one-line description:
- [pretender]: mode, languages, exclude
- [thresholds]: all app-level metric limits
- [thresholds.test], [thresholds.library], [thresholds.script]: role overrides
- [bands]: cyclomatic and cognitive band thresholds (green/yellow/red)
- [scope]: diff_only, diff_base
- [roles.*]: path glob matchers per role
- [execute], [plugins], [output]: advanced sections

## Additional sections
- Role detection: how pretender assigns roles (pragma → configured glob → file-name heuristic → app); pragma syntax: '# pretender: role=<name>' or '// pretender: role=<name>'
- History and feedback loop: what .pretender/events.jsonl stores, how hotspot and pattern hints are computed and displayed, and the 90-day retention window

## Verification
Cross-check all documented keys against the struct fields in config.rs — every public field must have a corresponding row.

title: Document external plugin format and authoring guide
description:
The external plugin system (external_plugin.rs) is undocumented. Users need to know how to write and install custom metric plugins.

## Location
Place at docs/plugins.md. Add a 'Writing plugins' link in the root README.

## Content to cover
- Plugin manifest format: command, args, applies_to (file extensions), output_format (json-array or json-lines), and findings schema (source, code, line, message fields)
- Install location: ~/.config/pretender/metrics/<name>.toml
- How findings from plugins are merged into pretender check output
- Worked example: a complete, runnable minimal plugin — a TOML manifest plus a shell script that emits one JSON finding — committed under metrics/example/

## Verification
The example plugin in metrics/example/ can be installed and pretender check runs without crashing; the finding appears in --format human output.

title: Add per-language documentation pages
description:
Each language supported by pretender (Python, Rust, Go, JavaScript, TypeScript, Java, Ruby, C, C++) has a directory under languages/ with only metrics.scm and plugin.toml. These need human-readable documentation.

## Location
Place each page at languages/<lang>/README.md. Add a 'Language support' link in the root README pointing to languages/.

## Required sections (all 9 pages must follow this template)
- Overview: one-line description of the adapter
- Tracked nodes: which tree-sitter node types map to branches, calls, assignments
- Assertion patterns: which call names are recognised as assertions
- Quirks and limitations: anything surprising or not yet supported
- Example output: a short pretender check snippet showing real metric values

## Verification
All 9 README.md files exist, follow the same section order, and the root README links to languages/.

title: External metric plugin wrappers (eslint, ruff, clippy, staticcheck)
description: Implement metric plugin runner: read ~/.config/pretender/metrics/*.toml, for each applicable plugin run 'command' template with {files} substituted, parse output via 'parser' (json/text), map fields via 'mapping' to normalised issue format. Wire into pretender check output same as native metrics. Test with ruff (Python) and clippy (Rust) which are already installed.
notes: Verify: pretender check tests/fixtures/python_simple.py --format=json output contains at least one finding with source='ruff'. Run ruff on the fixture first to confirm it produces findings. Also verify clippy: pretender check src/ --format=json contains at least one finding with source='clippy' when the project has clippy warnings.
close_reason: Implemented external metric plugin runner: load ~/.config/pretender/metrics/*.toml, run command with {files} expansion, parse json/json-lines output via dot-notation field mapping. Ruff integration test passes. 111 tests.

title: Mutation testing wrapper (pretender mutation)
description: pretender mutation [paths]: wrap per-language mutation tools (Stryker for JS/TS, PIT for Java, mutmut for Python, cargo-mutants for Rust). Plugin-style: each language plugin declares mutation_tool in plugin.toml. Run tool, parse output, normalise into standard report. Default threshold: 60% mutation score. Output: surviving mutants sorted by impact with test names that missed them.
notes: HITL: mutation test runs are long (5–60 min depending on codebase size); do not run in CI without explicit [execute] enabled = true in pretender.toml. When implementing, verify with --dry-run flag first: pretender mutation tests/fixtures/python_simple.py --dry-run must list planned mutants without running them. Output must be normalised into the same FindingReport struct used by pretender check — surviving mutants sorted by file/line, printed with test names that missed them.
close_reason: mutation command implemented with 31 unit tests + 1 CLI test; dry-run, threshold, cargo-mutants/mutmut/stryker parsers

title: Diff-only mode: git2 integration for staged files and diff-base
description: Use git2 crate to: (1) --staged: list staged files from git index, (2) --diff-only: list files changed relative to diff_base ref (default: origin/main from config). Only pass the filtered file list to the scan engine. This is critical for hook performance — must be <2s even on large repos.
close_reason: Implemented: git2 integration for --staged (diff index vs HEAD) and --diff-only/--diff-base (diff tree to tree). Hooked into CheckArgs::run(), apply_file_filter() with canonical path matching. Updated pre-commit hook template to use --staged. 4 new integration tests.

title: Structural duplication detection (pretender duplication)
description: Within-file structural clone detection via normalised AST subtree hashing. Algorithm: (1) walk tree-sitter CST, (2) for each subtree ≥10 nodes produce normalised hash: identifiers→$VAR, literals→$LIT_<type>, strip whitespace/comments, (3) group by hash, report pairs with ≥2 occurrences. Output: clone locations + similarity score 0-100. V0: within-file only. V1: cross-file with --cross-file flag.
close_reason: Implemented within-file structural clone detection via normalised AST subtree hashing. New duplication.rs module with 5 unit tests; CLI wired and smoke-tested.

title: CLI: pretender explain <metric>
description: pretender explain <metric>: print what the metric measures, the formula or definition, the threshold default and its citation source. Example: pretender explain cyclomatic prints McCabe 1976 definition, 1+branches formula, default threshold 10 with SonarQube citation. Deferred to V2 — no implementation needed for MVP. Create the issue now to capture intent.
close_reason: implemented: static catalog of 11 metrics, print_doc formatter, error with available list; 49 tests green
